mcqData.text: A partner whose liability is unlimited but plays no key role in management?
mcqData.explanation:
Passive partners have unlimited liability unless the firm is registered as "Limited."

Correct Answer: Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Pinky, Rinky, Jannat. Pinky plays the main role in management. Q: Pinky is what type of partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Active/General (Sadharon) partners manage the firm and have unlimited liability.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Rinky goes insane (insanity). Q: Method of dissolution?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44(a) states a court may dissolve a firm if a partner becomes of unsound mind.

Correct Answer: By Court order

mcqData.text: In which document are the internal rules of a company written? (Note: Contextual Outlier)
mcqData.explanation:
Though a company question, it appears in some mixed board sets. Articles of Association (Shonghovidhi) manage internal rules.

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: What is mentioned in Section 58 of the Partnership Act 1932?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 58 details the procedure for the "Registration" (Nibondhon) of a firm.

Correct Answer: Registration

mcqData.text: Stem: Mrs. Amena, Saleha & sisters. Sokhina is skilled and taken as a partner without capital. Q: Sokhina is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
A "Worker Partner" (Kormi) provides skills/labor instead of capital.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Saleha failed to transfer her share to her sister. Q: Why? (i. Lack of trust, ii. Difficulty in transfer, iii. Unlimited liability)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership shares cannot be transferred to outsiders without consent of all partners.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which partnership is dissolved via a written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 covers dissolution of "Partnership at Will" (Oichhik) via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional

mcqData.text: Whose consent is needed to change the terms of a partnership deed?
mcqData.explanation:
Any change in the deed requires the unanimous consent of all partners.

Correct Answer: All Partners

mcqData.text: What acts as the guideline/director for managing a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro) is the operational manual of the firm.

Correct Answer: Deed

mcqData.text: A minor can be which type of partner?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors have liability limited to their share only.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which partner manages but does not invest capital?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Worker partners provide labor, not funds.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Essential characteristics: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory/essential legal requirement for existence.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution occurs if- (i. Partner goes insane, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Business is illegal)
mcqData.explanation:
Insanity is a court-ordered ground (Sec 44), not compulsory (Sec 41).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partnership is managed by- (i. All, ii. One on behalf of all, iii. Max capital provider)
mcqData.explanation:
This reflects the principle of Mutual Agency (Sec 4).

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which is the core document of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat for Emphasis) The Deed is the primary document.

Correct Answer: Deed

mcqData.text: Max members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
General limit is 20; banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 20

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Under the 1932 Act, a partnership must have at least 2 members.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Which of these are considered business organizations? (i. Star Club Ltd, ii. ABC Poultry, iii. Polly Nursing Home)
mcqData.explanation:
Profit-oriented production or service entities are all business organizations.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partners must be- (i. Educated, ii. Of sound mind, iii. Adult)
mcqData.explanation:
Legally, a partner must be a major (adult) and sane. Education is not a requirement.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are only admitted for benefits with limited liability.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: For which business is registration NOT mandatory?
mcqData.explanation:
Registration of a partnership is optional in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Which business has the highest liability risk?
mcqData.explanation:
Partners have "Joint and Several" unlimited liability, making it riskier than a sole trader.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Rana, Mona, Kabir (10k, 15k, 20k). No profit ratio in deed. Profit: 48,000. Q: Rana's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent deed = Equal sharing. 48,000 / 3 = 16,000.

Correct Answer: 16,000

mcqData.text: Prevailing Partnership Law in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
(Standard Answer) The 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: What is the core basis of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat for Emphasis) Contract (Chukti) is the basis.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: What can cause the dissolution of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 42(c) states the death of a partner dissolves the firm (unless otherwise agreed).

Correct Answer: Death of partner

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat) Minimum is 2.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Main basis of partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
While trust is the 'soul', the legal basis is always the 'Contract'.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: A partnership agreement can be- (i. Oral, ii. Written, iii. Registered)
mcqData.explanation:
All three forms are valid under the 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partnership is better than Sole trader because- (i. More capital, ii. Collective decision, iii. Owner's freedom)
mcqData.explanation:
Owners lose individual freedom in a partnership as decisions are shared.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: Jamil & Jahid admit deceased partner Joynul's minor son. Q: The son is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors = Limited (Seemaboddho).

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Why was the minor admitted? (i. To provide benefits, ii. To keep firm's condition, iii. To consume his assets)
mcqData.explanation:
Taking a minor is for their benefit, not for consuming their assets.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: W, X, Y, Z formed a firm under 1932 Act. Q: What type of ownership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
Mention of the 1932 Act confirms it's a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: W, X (Capital + Mgmt), Y (Experience, No Capital), Z (Name for fee). Q: Partner types? (i. General, ii. Worker, iii. Nominal)
mcqData.explanation:
W, X are General; Y is Worker; Z is Nominal.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Main basis of partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) "Contract" is the root.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are Limited partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Firm introduces Mr. X as a partner. He stays silent despite knowing it. Q: What type of partner is Mr. X?
mcqData.explanation:
Partner by Estoppel (Protibondh) occurs when someone knowingly stays silent while being represented as a partner.

Correct Answer: Estoppel

mcqData.text: With which business is the word 'Contract' (Chukti) most associated?
mcqData.explanation:
Contract is the legal foundation of a partnership (Section 5, 1932 Act).

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Ahsan & 10 friends. Ahsan agrees on the condition of liability limited to 3L. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
A firm where at least one partner has limited liability is a Limited (Seemaboddho) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Under which section does a partnership lose legal benefits if not registered?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 69 details the legal consequences/disabilities of non-registration.

Correct Answer: 69

mcqData.text: Whose consent is needed to change deed conditions?
mcqData.explanation:
Unanimous consent of all partners is required for any deed modification.

Correct Answer: Partners

mcqData.text: Who participates in business without supplying capital?
mcqData.explanation:
Worker (Kormi) partners contribute labor/skill instead of financial capital.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: If 2 out of 3 partners are declared bankrupt, the dissolution will be-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 states a firm MUST dissolve if all but one partner become insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Stem: Abdur Rahim & 3 friends start a garment factory based on mutual understanding. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple people starting a business via understanding/contract is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Hiring large unemployed workforce. Q: Which environment factor?
mcqData.explanation:
Availability of human resources/labor is a key social environment factor.

Correct Answer: Social

mcqData.text: A court can dissolve a partnership if-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44(d) allows court dissolution if a partner willfully/persistently breaks the agreement.

Correct Answer: Persistent breach of deed

mcqData.text: Stem: Kayes & Himel (90k, 60k). Later Masud joins (50k). No mention of profit ratio. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Ownership shared among multiple partners is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 42,000. Q: How much profit will Masud get?
mcqData.explanation:
If the deed is silent, profit is shared EQUALLY. (42,000 / 3 = 14,000).

Correct Answer: 14,000

mcqData.text: Which partner participates in management?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Active partners take charge of daily operations.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution happens when- (i. Insanity, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Illegal business)
mcqData.explanation:
Insanity is a ground for Court dissolution (Section 44), not Compulsory (Section 41).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Standard Answer) Minors are Limited (Seemaboddho) partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Regarding Limited Partnerships- (i. All limited, ii. At least one limited, iii. At least two unlimited)
mcqData.explanation:
Law requires at least one general (unlimited) partner in a limited firm. (Options are slightly tricky, check Board standard).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: If profit-sharing ratio is not in the deed, it must be distributed-
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Distribution Rule.

Correct Answer: Equally

mcqData.text: Which are NOT characteristics of a partnership? (i. Contractual, ii. Certainty of stability, iii. Legal entity)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership lacks stability and separate legal entity status.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: Inheritance business (children sharing profit) is not partnership because-
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership must arise from a contract (Section 5), not inheritance.

Correct Answer: No contractual relation

mcqData.text: Stem: Kamal, Bimal, Peter. Bimal dies. They admit his minor son Amal. Q: Amal's type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are always Limited partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Amal (minor) is admitted after his father Bimal's death. Q: What is the objective of keeping the minor? (i. Providing benefits, ii. Keeping business size, iii. Developing as a businessman)
mcqData.explanation:
Minors are primarily admitted for their benefit and to gain business experience.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which partnership dissolves via a simple written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 states 'Partnership at Will' (Oichhik) can be dissolved by any partner via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Which partner invests capital AND participates in management?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Active (Sadharon) partners handle both funds and daily operations.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Characteristics of Limited Partnership: (i. Has unlimited partners, ii. Has limited partners, iii. Uses 'Ltd.' in name)
mcqData.explanation:
Unlike companies, limited partnerships do not use "Ltd." at the end of their name.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Prevailing Partnership Law in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The governing law is the Partnership Act of 1932.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Which business has no separate legal entity?
mcqData.explanation:
Legally, a firm and its partners are considered the same entity.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Lalu, Bhulu & Nilu. Lalu manages the firm. Bhulu & Nilu are bankrupt. Q: What type is Lalu?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who manages and has unlimited liability is a General/Active partner.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) How will the firm dissolve since 2 out of 3 are bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 states a firm MUST dissolve if all but one partner become insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Partners must be- (i. Adult, ii. Educated, iii. Of sound mind)
mcqData.explanation:
Legal capacity requires adulthood and sanity, not a specific education level.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Banking partnership limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: Stem: Jamil & Shamim invest 2.5L and 5L based on mutual understanding. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Ownership shared by 2 people via contract is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 1.2L. No ratio in deed. Q: Jamil's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit sharing. 1,20,000 / 2 = 60,000.

Correct Answer: 60,000

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Contract, ii. Multiple members, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory legal requirement for existence.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who allows the firm to use their name/reputation?
mcqData.explanation:
Nominal (Nammatro) partners lend their name for a fee or goodwill.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: What plays the most role in smooth management?
mcqData.explanation:
While contract is the basis, Trust (Saddbishshash) ensures smooth running.

Correct Answer: Mutual trust & confidence

mcqData.text: Stem: 3 friends form a firm to build a building. After profit, they decide to continue. Q: First firm type?
mcqData.explanation:
Formed for a specific task (building construction) is a Specific (Bishes) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Specific

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) What is the liability of the partners in the continued firm?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership liability is always unlimited (Oshim) unless registered as 'Limited'.

Correct Answer: Unlimited

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: A Limited partnership is one where-
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partnership must have at least one partner with limited liability.

Correct Answer: At least 1 limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Shamshul, Morsheda, Najmun & Badrun. Shamshul does not participate in mgmt. Q: Shamshul is?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital but not managing makes one a Passive (Nishkriyo) partner.

Correct Answer: Passive/Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: 4 partners. 3 invest 3L each, 1 is an engineer with no capital. No profit ratio in deed. Profit: 6L. Q: How much will Badrun get?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit sharing. 6,00,000 / 4 = 1,50,000.

Correct Answer: 1.5 Lakh

mcqData.text: Which section of the 1932 Act defines "Dissolution"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 39 defines the dissolution of a firm (Bilopsadhon).

Correct Answer: 39

mcqData.text: Stem: Abid, Akash, Akib are partners. Friend Shohel is taken for his reputation/conduct with equal profit. Q: Shohel's type?
mcqData.explanation:
Taking a partner for name/reputation usage defines a Nominal (Nammatro) partner.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Net profit 44,000. Q: Shohel's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Equal profit sharing for 4 friends: 44,000 / 4 = 11,000.

Correct Answer: 11,000

mcqData.text: Who is considered a "Real/True" partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners are real partners, whereas others like 'By Conduct' are only liable by law.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which business is most risky regarding liability?
mcqData.explanation:
Partners have unlimited liability, making it the most risky among options.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Who is eligible to be a partner?
mcqData.explanation:
A sane adult housewife is legally capable of entering a contract.

Correct Answer: Housewife

mcqData.text: Stem: Johny, Rony, Sabbir (1L, 1.4L, 2L). They agree to share profit based on capital ratio. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple owners sharing profit/loss via agreement is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit: 44,000. Q: Sabbir's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Capital ratio = 10:14:20 or 5:7:10. Total parts = 22. Sabbir gets (44,000 / 22) * 10 = 20,000.

Correct Answer: 20,000

mcqData.text: Stem: X, Y, Z (25k, 35k, 50k). Profit sharing by capital ratio. Q: Z's share if profit is 22,000?
mcqData.explanation:
Ratio = 25:35:50 or 5:7:10. Total = 22. Z gets (22,000 / 22) * 10 = 10,000.

Correct Answer: 10,000

mcqData.text: What is the full form of SME?
mcqData.explanation:
Small and Medium Enterprise is the correct term.

Correct Answer: Small & Medium Enterprise

mcqData.text: Which partner can claim profit from the business?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners have a right to profit; Quasi-partners only get interest on their loan.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which section covers 'Dissolution by Notice'?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 covers dissolution of partnership at will by notice.

Correct Answer: 43

mcqData.text: Stem: Arita, Adiba (invest + mgmt), Jumaina (invest only, no time). Q: Jumaina is?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital but not participating in management defines a Sleeping (Supto) partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping/Dormant

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) In which ratio will profit be shared (since ratio is not in deed)?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit (1:1:1).

Correct Answer: 1 : 1 : 1

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory legal essential.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which section defines "Partnership at Will"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 7 defines 'Oichhik' partnership.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: Stem: A & B start a 5-year firm. After 5 years, they continue. Q: What type is it now?
mcqData.explanation:
Continuing a specific-term firm after its term expires makes it "Partnership at Will".

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) They have a dispute of 35k with a debtor. Q: How can they recover? (i. Court, ii. Trade Assoc, iii. Negotiation)
mcqData.explanation:
Unregistered firms cannot sue for >100 Taka in court; they must use negotiation/arbitration.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What works as the guideline/director for managing a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro) contains all rules for operations.

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Stem: Erika & 4 friends rent stalls for 2 months for a Trade Fair. Erika didn't participate in mgmt. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Formed for a specific location (Trade Fair) or duration is a Special/Specific (Bishes) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Special

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 5L. Ratio not set. Erika wants equal share. Q: Erika's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Erika & 4 friends = 5 people. Silent ratio means equal sharing (1/5 each).

Correct Answer: One-fifth

mcqData.text: What is the core basis of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Contract (Chukti) is the legal soul of partnership.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: K, Kh, G start a firm. No profit ratio in agreement. Q: If the agreement is written, it's called?
mcqData.explanation:
A written partnership agreement is specifically called a "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro).

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) A debtor owes 40k but firm can't act. Q: Steps needed to avoid this? (i. Written deed, ii. Registration, iii. Appointing arbitrator)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is required to sue a third party for debts over 100 Taka.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Similarity between Sole Proprietorship and Partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
In both, owners have unlimited (Oshim) liability for business debts.

Correct Answer: Unlimited Liability

mcqData.text: Stem: Rajib, Liton, Masud. Masud bankrupt. Firm dissolves. Only Rajib's personal property seized. Q: Rajib is?
mcqData.explanation:
Active/General partners have unlimited liability extending to personal assets.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) How will the firm dissolve since Masud is bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41: Dissolution is compulsory if any partner becomes insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Stem: Suha & Ayana (3L & 4L capital) start a business. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple owners (2) with capital investment is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: P, Q, R admit Mr. S as a Limited Partner. S is happy. Q: Main reason for S's happiness?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners only risk their invested capital, not personal assets.

Correct Answer: Limited liability

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Reason for taking S? (i. New capital, ii. No mgmt complexity, iii. Less profit)
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners provide capital but cannot interfere in management.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Partner who retires but doesn't withdraw capital (leaves it as a loan)?
mcqData.explanation:
A Quasi-partner (Apato-drishtite) leaves their capital as a loan in the firm.

Correct Answer: Quasi-partner

mcqData.text: Businesses with Unlimited Liability: (i. Sole, ii. Cooperative, iii. Partnership)
mcqData.explanation:
Cooperatives have limited liability; Sole and Partnership are unlimited.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Stem: Fahim manages the firm but doesn't provide capital. Q: Fahim is?
mcqData.explanation:
Worker (Kormi) partners provide labor/skill instead of money.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Roni, Joni, Soni. Soni provides capital but doesn't manage. Q: Soni's type?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital without management makes one a Sleeping partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping/Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Jahid, Shofiq, Anwar (Partners). Q: Core basis of their business?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Contract (Chukti) is the foundation.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Best way to solve long-term issues?
mcqData.explanation:
A clear "Partnership Deed" is the best way to prevent and solve disputes.

Correct Answer: Deed/Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: Maruf (Sales Rep) introduces himself as a partner. Q: Maruf is?
mcqData.explanation:
A person who acts or speaks as if they are a partner is "By Conduct" (Achorone Onumito).

Correct Answer: By Conduct

mcqData.text: Stem: Siam starts a shop alone. Later takes friend Nirjon. Q: Initially, what was it?
mcqData.explanation:
A business owned and run by one person is Sole Proprietorship (Ek-malikana).

Correct Answer: Sole Proprietorship

mcqData.text: Stem: Siam starts a shop with 1L capital. Later takes Nirjon with 60k. Profit: 20,000. Q: How much profit will Nirjon receive?
mcqData.explanation:
Since the stem doesn't mention a profit-sharing ratio, profit is shared EQUALLY (1:1). 20,000 / 2 = 10,000.

Correct Answer: 10,000

mcqData.text: A partner who doesn't invest capital but actively uses their expertise/intellect in business?
mcqData.explanation:
A Worker Partner (Kormi) provides labor or specialized skill/intellect instead of money.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
According to the 1932 Act, a partnership must have at least 2 members.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: How many ways can a partnership business be dissolved?
mcqData.explanation:
According to Sections 40-44 of the 1932 Act, there are 5 methods of dissolution.

Correct Answer: 5

mcqData.text: Even if children run the business after a person's death, it is not called a partnership. Why?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership is based on a contract (Section 5), not on status or inheritance.

Correct Answer: Absence of contractual relationship

mcqData.text: Stem: Tina, Mila, and Rubi start a business. Capitals: 1L, 1.4L, and 2L. Q: What type of business is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A business formed by multiple people based on an agreement is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) If the profit is 75,000, how much profit will Rubi receive?
mcqData.explanation:
If the ratio is not mentioned in the deed, profit is shared equally (75,000 ÷ 3).

Correct Answer: 25,000

mcqData.text: If it's impossible to maintain the minimum of 2 members, what type of dissolution occurs?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 41, if members drop to 1, the business must be dissolved compulsorily.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Which subject is mentioned in Section 58 of the Partnership Act of 1932?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 58 describes the procedure for registration of a firm.

Correct Answer: Registration

mcqData.text: Stem: Sohag does not participate in management. Q: What type of partner is Sohag?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who provides capital but does not manage is a Sleeping/Passive partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) How will the firm dissolve due to Sohag's death?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 42(c), the death of a partner causes dissolution as a special event.

Correct Answer: Subject to special event

mcqData.text: Which Partnership Act is currently in force in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The Partnership Act of 1932 is the governing law in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Which partnership is dissolved by a simple written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 states a Partnership at Will can be dissolved by any partner via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Registration, iii. Contractual relationship)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory or essential element for the existence of a firm.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Reasons for Compulsory Dissolution: (i. Illegal act, ii. Insolvency, iii. Death)
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41: Death is a special event, not a cause for compulsory dissolution.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Reasons for creating a partnership: (i. Capital shortage, ii. Personal limits, iii. Large scale benefits)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership is formed to overcome the limitations of sole proprietorship.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Due to which of the following can a case NOT be filed in the name of the firm?
mcqData.explanation:
Without a separate legal entity, a firm cannot sue or be sued in its own name.

Correct Answer: Lack of separate entity

mcqData.text: In which country did Limited Partnership originate?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partnerships (Commenda) originated in medieval Italy.

Correct Answer: Italy

mcqData.text: Stem: Shamshun did not participate in management. Q: What type of partner is Shamshun?
mcqData.explanation:
Because she does not manage, she is a Passive/Sleeping partner.

Correct Answer: Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Badrun (Engineer) gave no capital. Profit is 6L. Q: How much profit will Badrun get?
mcqData.explanation:
With 4 partners and no deed ratio, profit is divided equally (6 ÷ 4).

Correct Answer: 1.5 Lakh

mcqData.text: Dissolution by the Court can occur when-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44 allows the court to dissolve a firm for persistent misconduct.

Correct Answer: Persistent breach of contract

mcqData.text: Stem: Jahed & 3 friends start a firm for a 2-year term. Q: What type of partnership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A partnership formed for a fixed term (2 years) is a Particular/Specific Time partnership.

Correct Answer: Specific period

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Debt: 3,00,000. 4 partners in total. Q: How much debt should Jahed pay?
mcqData.explanation:
Silence in the deed means equal sharing. 3,00,000 ÷ 4 = 75,000 (Option B is the closest round figure).

Correct Answer: 1,00,000

mcqData.text: Stem: Shimul, Limon, and Abid. Shimul manages the firm. Limon and Abid are declared bankrupt by court. Q: What type of partner is Shimul?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who manages and has unlimited liability is a General/Active partner.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) How will the business be dissolved since Limon and Abid are bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 41, if all but one partner become insolvent, dissolution is compulsory.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Registration of a partnership business is—
mcqData.explanation:
According to the 1932 Act, registration is optional, though non-registration has disadvantages.

Correct Answer: Optional

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution happens when— (i. Business is illegal, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Death of a member)
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 defines compulsory dissolution; death (Sec 42) is a special event.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who is eligible to be a partner?
mcqData.explanation:
A housewife who is an adult and of sound mind is legally capable of entering a contract.

Correct Answer: Housewife

mcqData.text: What is the maximum number of members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The maximum limit for a general partnership is 20 members.

Correct Answer: 20

mcqData.text: Who does NOT have to carry unlimited liability?
mcqData.explanation:
A limited partner's liability is restricted to their invested capital.

Correct Answer: Limited Partner

mcqData.text: Stem: X, Y, Z were partners. Y died. X and Z admitted Y's minor son. Q: What is Y's son called?
mcqData.explanation:
Under the law, a minor admitted to the benefits of a firm is treated as a limited partner.

Correct Answer: Limited Partner

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) The purpose of keeping the minor as a partner is— (i. Benefits, ii. Continuity, iii. Development)
mcqData.explanation:
Minors are primarily admitted for their benefit and to train them as future businessmen.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: If 2 out of 3 partners are declared bankrupt, the dissolution will be—
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) If all but one are insolvent, it must close under Section 41.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Mutual Agency (Representation) exists in—
mcqData.explanation:
Mutual Agency is a core feature where each partner is both principal and agent.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Partnership matters include: (i. Mandatory capital, ii. Written/Registered deed, iii. Mutual trust)
mcqData.explanation:
Capital isn't always mandatory (Worker partner) and deeds can be oral; Trust is essential.

Correct Answer: iii

mcqData.text: What is the relationship between partners called?
mcqData.explanation:
"Partnership" is the name of the relationship between persons who have agreed to share profits.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Which feature makes a partner liable for the acts of another partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Due to Mutual Agency, an act done by one partner binds the whole firm.

Correct Answer: Mutual Agency

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual rel., iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration (iii) is a formality, not an "essential element" for formation.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Akib and friends signed a contract for 3 years. What type of partnership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A partnership formed for a fixed duration (3 years) is a Particular Partnership.

Correct Answer: Particular/Specific

mcqData.text: Which type of shareholders receive dividends before others?
mcqData.explanation:
Preference (Ogrodhikar) shareholders have a prior claim on profits.

Correct Answer: Preference Share

mcqData.text: What is the minimum number of directors in a Public Ltd. Co.?
mcqData.explanation:
Under the 1994 Act, a Public Ltd Co. must have at least 3 directors.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Which document is considered the "Birth Certificate" of a Public Ltd. Co.?
mcqData.explanation:
The Certificate of Incorporation (Nibondhonpotro) gives the company legal life.

Correct Answer: Incorporation Certificate

mcqData.text: Within how many days must minimum capital be collected after issuing a prospectus?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 148: A company must collect minimum subscription within 120 days.

Correct Answer: 120

mcqData.text: Which type of shares must one buy to become a Director?
mcqData.explanation:
Directors must hold "Qualification Shares," which are usually Ordinary/General shares.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: For which type of share is no payment required?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are issued for free to existing shareholders from retained earnings.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Which letter is called the "Birth Certificate" of the company?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Incorporation marks the legal birth of the entity.

Correct Answer: Incorporation Certificate

mcqData.text: Stem: 'Swapno Fashion' formed under 1994 Act. Mr. Tareq bought shares that allow him to be a director. Q: What type of organization?
mcqData.explanation:
Organizations formed under the 1994 Act with shares are Joint Stock Companies.

Correct Answer: Public Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of aBOVE) What type of shares did Mr. Tareq purchase?
mcqData.explanation:
Only General (Ordinary) shareholders usually have the right to become directors.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Which section of the Company Act defines a Joint Stock Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 2(1)(d) of the 1994 Act provides the legal definition.

Correct Answer: 2 (1-d)

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a Private Ltd. Company?
mcqData.explanation:
A Private Ltd. Co. requires a minimum of 2 members.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Stem: Miss Nishat is a shareholder. Someone sued her institution in court. Q: Which feature is highlighted by the lawsuit?
mcqData.explanation:
Like a human, a company can sue or be sued in its own name due to its "Artificial Person" status.

Correct Answer: Artificial Person

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What applies to Nishat's shares (Preference)? (i. Can manage, ii. Capital refund priority, iii. No voting right)
mcqData.explanation:
Preference shareholders cannot participate in management or vote for directors.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What is Schedule-1 (Tafsil-1)?
mcqData.explanation:
Schedule-1 can act as a substitute for the Articles of Association in certain cases.

Correct Answer: Memorandum

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Private Ltd. Company?
mcqData.explanation:
A Private Ltd. Co. must have at least 2 directors.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Stem: 'XY' Co. manager prefers a system where investors get fixed interest instead of ownership. Q: How is capital raised?
mcqData.explanation:
Debentures (Rinnopotro) provide fixed interest, not ownership.

Correct Answer: Debenture

mcqData.text: Stem: Hasan & 10 friends form a firm. Later they decide to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
A company started by friends (limited members) is initially a Private Ltd.

Correct Answer: Private Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To implement the new decision (selling shares to public), they must- (i. Increase members, ii. Issue prospectus, iii. Get commencement letter)
mcqData.explanation:
Converting to Public Ltd. requires more members, a prospectus, and a Commencement Letter.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Which is a general characteristic of a Joint Stock business?
mcqData.explanation:
A "Common Seal" (Shidhoron Shilmohor) acts as the signature of the artificial person.

Correct Answer: Common Seal

mcqData.text: What type of company was the East India Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Chartered (Sodonprapto) companies were formed by royal decree (like East India Co.).

Correct Answer: Chartered

mcqData.text: From which type of organization does the government receive the most revenue?
mcqData.explanation:
Large scale operations and high corporate tax rates make companies the top revenue source.

Correct Answer: Joint Stock Company

mcqData.text: Selling shares is better than selling debentures because: (i. No need to refund before liquidation, ii. No interest if no profit, iii. No invite to AGM)
mcqData.explanation:
Shares don't require fixed interest or mandatory repayment like debt, but shareholders must be invited to the AGM.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a legal characteristic of a company?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited liability is the primary legal protection offered by the corporate structure.

Correct Answer: Limited liability

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Jiban wants to build a firm that owns >50% shares of another firm and appoints its directors. Q: What type of company is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A Holding Company controls others by owning more than half of their shares.

Correct Answer: Holding

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Reason for public trust in such institutions: (i. Limited liability, ii. Perpetual existence, iii. Transferable shares)
mcqData.explanation:
All three are core strengths of a Joint Stock Company that attract investors.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Law requires at least 3 directors for Public Ltd.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Shafi & 8 friends initiate 'Food Processing Pvt Ltd.' Q: What is Mr. Shafi's primary identity here?
mcqData.explanation:
People who take the initiative to form a company are called Promoters (Probortok).

Correct Answer: Promoter

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What must Mr. Shafi do to start the firm? (i. Registration, ii. Collect minimum capital, iii. Financial planning)
mcqData.explanation:
Private Ltd companies do not need to collect "Minimum Subscription" from the public to start.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Selling shares is better than selling debentures because: (i. No refund before liquidation, ii. No interest if no profit, iii. No invite to AGM)
mcqData.explanation:
Identical to Q22. Debt (debentures) creates a legal obligation to pay interest regardless of profit.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which company document must be published in a newspaper?
mcqData.explanation:
A Prospectus (Biboronpotro) is a public invitation and must be advertised.

Correct Answer: Prospectus

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
(Frequent Question) Always 3 for Public, 2 for Private.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Advantages of company organization: (i. Autocratic leadership, ii. Skilled mgmt, iii. Limited liability)
mcqData.explanation:
Companies use democratic/skilled management, not autocratic.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: For which type of share is no payment required?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are gifts to existing shareholders from company reserves.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Stem: Altaf & 20 friends have a firm. They can't start work despite registration because a document is missing. Q: Which document?
mcqData.explanation:
Public Ltd companies cannot start business without a Certificate of Commencement (Karjarmbher Onumitipotro).

Correct Answer: Commencement Letter

mcqData.text: Stem: Co. 'M' buys all shares of Co. 'N' and takes over management. Q: What type of company is 'N'?
mcqData.explanation:
The controlled company is called the Subsidiary (Subsidiarity).

Correct Answer: Subsidiary

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Reason for Co. 'M's decision: (i. Expansion, ii. Profit increase, iii. Friendship)
mcqData.explanation:
Acquisitions are strategic business moves, not for "friendship."

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which capital must a Public Ltd Co. collect to get the Commencement Letter?
mcqData.explanation:
Minimum Subscription (Nunnotomo Muldhon) is a legal prerequisite for starting work.

Correct Answer: Minimum

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Samuel helped form a firm. He bought shares to cover preliminary expenses. Q: What type of shares?
mcqData.explanation:
Deferred (Bilombito) shares are usually held by promoters/founders.

Correct Answer: Deferred

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Features of this business: (i. Limited liability, ii. Mandatory registration, iii. Mutual agency)
mcqData.explanation:
Mutual Agency is for Partnerships; Companies have independent legal entity status.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which organization is governed by the 1994 Act?
mcqData.explanation:
The Companies Act of 1994 is the primary law for Joint Stock Companies in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: Company

mcqData.text: What is the third clause of the Memorandum of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
The clauses are: 1. Name, 2. Registered Office (Location), 3. Object, 4. Liability, 5. Capital, 6. Association.

Correct Answer: Object

mcqData.text: Sun Ltd' can enter contracts in its own name due to which feature?
mcqData.explanation:
Being an "Artificial Juridical Person" allows a company to sue, be sued, and sign contracts.

Correct Answer: Artificial Person

mcqData.text: Which is a short-term source of finance for a company?
mcqData.explanation:
Commercial bank loans are often short-term; shares and debentures are long-term capital.

Correct Answer: Commercial Bank

mcqData.text: Types of debentures a company usually issues: (i. Coupon bond, ii. Call bond, iii. Treasury bond)
mcqData.explanation:
Treasury bonds are issued by the government, not private joint-stock companies.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which is NOT mandatory to obtain a Commencement Letter?
mcqData.explanation:
Purchasing qualification shares is a director's duty but not an external filing for the letter itself.

Correct Answer: Qualification Shares

mcqData.text: Stem: 'Rimjhim Paint Ltd' advertises in a newspaper for capital after registration. Q: Min. directors?
mcqData.explanation:
Since they are raising capital from the public, they are a Public Ltd. Co., requiring 3 directors.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Regarding Rimjhim Paint Ltd: (i. Issued prospectus, ii. Is Private Ltd, iii. Must create Articles)
mcqData.explanation:
Public companies issue a prospectus and must have internal rules (Articles of Association).

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a clause of the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
Management rules belong to the "Articles of Association," not the "Memorandum."

Correct Answer: Management

mcqData.text: Which document is needed for day-to-day operations?
mcqData.explanation:
The Articles of Association (Porimel Niyamaboli) govern internal daily management.

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: Due to which feature does a Ltd. Co. operate in its own name?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Artificial personality gives the firm its own legal name and identity.

Correct Answer: Artificial Person

mcqData.text: Which institution can prepare a "Statement in lieu of Prospectus"?
mcqData.explanation:
If a Public Ltd. company raises capital privately, it issues a "Statement in lieu of Prospectus."

Correct Answer: Public Ltd

mcqData.text: What type of dissolution occurs if a firm fails to pay debts?
mcqData.explanation:
Failing to pay debts leads to compulsory (Badhyatomulok) winding up by the court.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Talha owns 500 shares (1k each) and gets a fixed dividend rate. Q: Share type?
mcqData.explanation:
Preference (Ogrodhikar) shares offer a fixed/predetermined rate of dividend.

Correct Answer: Preference

mcqData.text: Main source of raising company capital?
mcqData.explanation:
Selling shares is the primary method to raise equity capital.

Correct Answer: Share Sale

mcqData.text: For which document can "Schedule-1" be adopted?
mcqData.explanation:
If a Public Ltd. doesn't write its own Articles, it can adopt "Schedule-1" (Table A).

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: Which shares are issued to cover preliminary expenses?
mcqData.explanation:
Deferred (Bilombito) shares are often used to pay promoters for startup costs.

Correct Answer: Deferred

mcqData.text: Stem: Nuraj & 8 friends start a firm. Later, another big company buys 60% of their shares. Q: Current type?
mcqData.explanation:
Since 60% is owned by another firm, Nuraj’s firm is now a Subsidiary (Subsidiarity).

Correct Answer: Subsidiary

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Nuraj & friends in the firm: (i. Are Promoters, ii. Get deferred shares, iii. Have unlimited liability)
mcqData.explanation:
They took the initiative (Promoters) and founders often hold deferred shares; liability is limited.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Min. number of promoters required for a Private Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Private Ltd requires at least 2 people to start.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Which document must a Public Ltd get from the Registrar after registration?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Public companies need the Commencement Letter to actually start operations.

Correct Answer: Commencement Letter

mcqData.text: Which type of shareholders are the actual owners of the company?
mcqData.explanation:
General shareholders bear the ultimate risk and enjoy full voting rights, making them the true owners.

Correct Answer: General/Ordinary

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Bablu and friends form an artificial person with legal entity status. It can sell shares to raise capital. Q: Min. members?
mcqData.explanation:
Since it sells shares to the public, it is a Public Ltd. Co., which requires at least 7 members.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: What is limited in a Public Limited Company?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Limited" in the name refers to the fact that shareholders are only liable for the face value of their shares.

Correct Answer: Liability of shareholders

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a clause of the Memorandum of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
Ownership is not a clause; the clauses are Name, Location, Object, Liability, Capital, and Association.

Correct Answer: Ownership

mcqData.text: Which of the following is considered the "Charter" (Sonod) for a company?
mcqData.explanation:
The Memorandum of Association (Smarkolipi) is the fundamental charter of a company.

Correct Answer: Memorandum

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Jony registered 'ACD' in Jan 2021 and got the commencement letter. Q: What type of organization is 'ACD'?
mcqData.explanation:
Only Public Ltd. companies require a "Certificate of Commencement" (কাজ শুরুর অনুমতি) to start.

Correct Answer: Public Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) The firm failed to start work within a year of getting the letter. Q: How will it be dissolved?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 241, failure to start business within a year is grounds for compulsory winding up.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: What is the main/primary document of a Joint Stock business?
mcqData.explanation:
The Memorandum is the constitution; everything else must follow its guidelines.

Correct Answer: Memorandum

mcqData.text: How many clauses are there in the Memorandum of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) There are 6 mandatory clauses defined in the Companies Act.

Correct Answer: 6

mcqData.text: What are the initiators of a company formation called?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Promoters (Probortok) take the initial steps to bring a company to life.

Correct Answer: Promoters

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Public Limited Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Public Ltd. needs 3 directors; Private Ltd. needs 2.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Stem: A creditor sues 'ABC Co. Ltd' for unpaid dues. Q: Which feature is highlighted by the firm being sued in its own name?
mcqData.explanation:
Artificial personality allows the firm to be treated as a legal individual in court.

Correct Answer: Artificial Person

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) The company issues shares instead of cash as a dividend. Q: What type of shares are these?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are "stock dividends" given to current owners instead of cash.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: What is included in the Articles of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
While the Memorandum mentions total capital, the Articles detail the classes and rules of capital.

Correct Answer: Capital matters

mcqData.text: What is the correct sequence of company formation? (i. Initiative -> Documents -> Registration -> Commencement)
mcqData.explanation:
The 4 stages are: 1. Promotion, 2. Documentation, 3. Incorporation, 4. Commencement.

Correct Answer: i

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Poltu and 15 friends form an artificial person. They later decide to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial type?
mcqData.explanation:
A company with friends (small group) that hasn't sold shares to the public yet is Private.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To implement the new decision (going public), they must: (i. Increase members, ii. Issue prospectus, iii. Get commencement letter)
mcqData.explanation:
Converting to a Public Ltd. company requires all three steps.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Which document contains the internal management rules?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Articles of Association = Internal management rules.

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: What is limited in a Public Limited Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Shareholders are only liable up to the amount of their share value.

Correct Answer: Shareholders' liability

mcqData.text: Which is issued to shareholders as proof of ownership?
mcqData.explanation:
A Share Certificate (শেয়ার সনদ) is the legal document proving a person owns shares.

Correct Answer: Share Certificate

mcqData.text: Which is the 4th clause of the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
The sequence is: 1. Name, 2. Location, 3. Object, 4. Liability, 5. Capital, 6. Association.

Correct Answer: Liability

mcqData.text: In which year was the Company Act enacted in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The Companies Act of 1994 is the current prevailing law in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: 1,994

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a Private Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Private Ltd needs min. 2; Public Ltd needs min. 7.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: What is the minimum % of shares the Govt must hold for a "Government Company"?
mcqData.explanation:
A Government Company must have at least 51% share capital owned by the government.

Correct Answer: 51%

mcqData.text: For a Public Ltd Co: (i. Min 7 members, ii. Max 50 members, iii. Can raise capital by selling shares)
mcqData.explanation:
Public Ltd has no upper limit on members (only limited by shares). Max 50 is for Private Ltd.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: A company is: (i. Law-created, ii. Perpetual existence, iii. Based on trust)
mcqData.explanation:
Companies are based on legal formalities and "Limited Liability," whereas partnerships are based on mutual trust.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: For which share is no payment required?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are issued for free to existing shareholders from accumulated profits.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Which year is the Company Act from?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Always remember the 1994 Act.

Correct Answer: 1994

mcqData.text: Memorandum is: (i. Constitution, ii. Main document, iii. Rules for internal mgmt)
mcqData.explanation:
Internal management rules are found in the Articles of Association, not the Memorandum.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Min 7 for Public, Min 2 for Private.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: (Duplicate of Q88) Current Company Law year?
mcqData.explanation:
1994

Correct Answer: 1994

mcqData.text: Stem: 8 friends form a co. and register it. They can't start work without a key document. Q: Which one?
mcqData.explanation:
Public companies must get the Certificate of Commencement to start business.

Correct Answer: Commencement Letter

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What type of firm is this?
mcqData.explanation:
Since it needs a Commencement Letter and has >7 members, it's a Public Ltd Co.

Correct Answer: Public Ltd

mcqData.text: How many stages are in company formation?
mcqData.explanation:
The 4 stages: Promotion, Documentation, Incorporation, Commencement.

Correct Answer: 4

mcqData.text: Why does a Public Ltd issue a Prospectus?
mcqData.explanation:
It is a public invitation specifically to sell shares and debentures.

Correct Answer: Sell shares

mcqData.text: Stem: Tarek & 12 friends have a firm with artificial personality. They want to change structure to raise more capital. Q: Initial type?
mcqData.explanation:
A legal entity with a small group of friends (13 total) is a Private Ltd.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To implement new decision (going Public): (i. Increase members, ii. Issue prospectus, iii. Get commencement letter)
mcqData.explanation:
All three are required to convert a Private Ltd to a Public Ltd and raise capital.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: From the perspective of source, how many types of company capital are there?
mcqData.explanation:
Generally categorized as Internal (Equity) and External (Debt/Loans).

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: When was the first Company Act passed (historical context)?
mcqData.explanation:
The Joint Stock Companies Act 1844/1845 was the first of its kind.

Correct Answer: 1845

mcqData.text: What type of company is Bangladesh Bank?
mcqData.explanation:
Statutory (বিধিবদ্ধ) companies are created by a special act of Parliament/President.

Correct Answer: Statutory

mcqData.text: If a company owns more than 50% shares of another company, it is called?
mcqData.explanation:
A "Holding Company" (নিয়ন্ত্রণকারী কোম্পানি) is one that controls another by owning >50% shares.

Correct Answer: Controlling/Holding Co.

mcqData.text: For which type of share is no payment required?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Bonus shares are free shares given to existing owners from profits.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Law requires 3 for Public and 2 for Private companies.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Why must a company collect "Minimum Subscription"?
mcqData.explanation:
Minimum subscription is raised to cover startup costs and initial working capital.

Correct Answer: Preliminary costs

mcqData.text: A Holding company must own at least what % of its Subsidiary?
mcqData.explanation:
To have control, the holding company must own at least 51% (majority) of shares.

Correct Answer: 51%

mcqData.text: Stem: Sotorupa Co. issues new shares and distributes them proportionally to existing owners. Q: Identify these shares.
mcqData.explanation:
"Right Shares" (অধিকারযোগ্য) are offered to existing shareholders before the public.

Correct Answer: Right (Adhikar)

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Regarding Right Shares: (i. It's a type of general share, ii. Given against dividends, iii. Given to old owners on priority)
mcqData.explanation:
Right shares are offered to existing owners to buy (not free like bonus) to maintain their ownership %.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: One can become a member of a company by: (i. Buying shares, ii. Signing the Memorandum, iii. Taking deposits)
mcqData.explanation:
Membership comes from owning shares or being a founding signatory. Deposits make you a creditor.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: What is meant by "Artificial Personality" of a company?
mcqData.explanation:
It means the company can sue, be sued, and hold property in its own name, like a person.

Correct Answer: Independent legal entity with rights

mcqData.text: Which clause of the Memorandum is like a signature/mark of the promoters?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Association Clause" (সম্মতি ধারা) contains the signatures and declarations of the founders.

Correct Answer: Association/Consent

mcqData.text: Stem: Nibir and 4 friends form an artificial legal entity. They now decide to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial type?
mcqData.explanation:
A firm with a small group (5 people) that is a legal entity is initially a Private Ltd company.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To sell shares in the market, they must: (i. Increase members, ii. Change Articles, iii. Get SEC approval)
mcqData.explanation:
Converting to Public Ltd requires increasing members (min 7), updating rules, and SEC permission.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: What is the nature of a company's existence?
mcqData.explanation:
"Perpetual Succession" (চিরন্তন অস্তিত্ব) means the company lives on even if owners die.

Correct Answer: Perpetual

mcqData.text: Debenture (debt) holders are the company's—
mcqData.explanation:
Debenture holders (ঋণপত্র গ্রহীতা) lend money to the firm, so they are Creditors (পাওনাদার).

Correct Answer: Creditors

mcqData.text: Which is the primary document of a company organization?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) The Memorandum (স্মারকলিপি) is the primary "constitution."

Correct Answer: Memorandum

mcqData.text: Which type of shares must one buy to become a Director?
mcqData.explanation:
Directors must buy "Qualification Shares" (যোগ্যতাসূচক শেয়ার) to prove their commitment.

Correct Answer: Qualification

mcqData.text: Stem: Mahbub buys 10 Lac TK worth of debentures. He tries to vote in the AGM but can't. Q: Why?
mcqData.explanation:
Debenture holders are lenders (creditors), not owners. Only owners (shareholders) can vote.

Correct Answer: No voting rights

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) If Mahbub wants to vote, he must:
mcqData.explanation:
Voting power is only tied to owning Shares, not debentures.

Correct Answer: Buy shares

mcqData.text: When excess reserves are distributed as shares instead of cash, it is called—
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) This is the definition of Bonus Shares.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Which clause of the Memorandum does NOT require court permission to change?
mcqData.explanation:
Changing the Capital Clause usually only requires a special resolution and notifying the Registrar.

Correct Answer: Capital

mcqData.text: Ms. Sathila buys 100 shares at 1,000 TK each. What is her liability at winding up?
mcqData.explanation:
Liability is limited to the face value of shares held (100 $\times$ 1,000 = 1,00,000).

Correct Answer: 1,00,000

mcqData.text: In which year was the [historic] Company Act enacted in [British] Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
While the current act is 1994, the historic 1913 Act was the foundation for nearly a century.

Correct Answer: 1913

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Legal requirement for a Public Ltd company is at least 7 members.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: What type of organization was the British 'East India Company'?
mcqData.explanation:
Companies created by a Royal Charter (King/Queen's order) are "Chartered" companies.

Correct Answer: Chartered

mcqData.text: Stem: BRB Co. requires 7% shares for a director. Mr. Mahim buys 8%. Q: What type of shares are these?
mcqData.explanation:
Shares bought to gain voting rights and directorship are General/Ordinary shares.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Reason for directors' shareholding requirement? (i. Responsibility, ii. Legal order, iii. Protecting interests)
mcqData.explanation:
Owning "Qualification Shares" ensures directors are legally compliant and personally invested.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Why is "Minimum Subscription" required for a Joint Stock business?
mcqData.explanation:
Min. subscription covers initial registration and startup costs (প্রাথমিক খরচ).

Correct Answer: Preliminary Costs

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Rashed gets dividends first if there is profit, but nothing if there's no profit. Q: What type of Preference share?
mcqData.explanation:
Non-Cumulative means if the dividend isn't paid this year, it isn't "saved" for the next.

Correct Answer: Non-Cumulative

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Co. has 1 crore shares. Mr. Rasel has 35%. How many more shares for assured directorship?
mcqData.explanation:
Usually, 51% (controlling stake) is 51L. He has 35L. 51L - 35L = 16,00,000.

Correct Answer: 16,00,000

mcqData.text: What is mentioned in the Articles of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
Articles (পরিমেল নিয়মাবলি) handle internal bylaws and day-to-day management.

Correct Answer: Daily internal rules

mcqData.text: Which clause of the Memorandum defines the limits of the company's power?
mcqData.explanation:
The Object Clause defines the scope and boundaries of what the company can do.

Correct Answer: Object

mcqData.text: Which is an example of a Statutory (বিধিবদ্ধ) Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Krishi Bank is created by a special parliamentary act, making it a Statutory firm.

Correct Answer: Krishi Bank

mcqData.text: Stem: Kamran and 5 friends form a legal entity. They now decide to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial type?
mcqData.explanation:
Small group (6 people) + artificial legal entity = Private Ltd company.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To raise more capital, Kamran must: (i. Inc. members, ii. Get commencement cert, iii. Update Memorandum)
mcqData.explanation:
All three stages are necessary to convert to a Public Ltd company and sell shares publicly.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Minimum directors in a Public Ltd Co?
mcqData.explanation:
Public Ltd companies must have at least 3 directors by law.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Which is a disadvantage of a Joint Stock Company?
mcqData.explanation:
Formation involves heavy legal paperwork, registration fees, and strict regulations.

Correct Answer: Complex Formation

mcqData.text: How many clauses are in the Memorandum of Association?
mcqData.explanation:
There are 6 mandatory clauses: Name, Location, Object, Liability, Capital, and Association.

Correct Answer: 6

mcqData.text: Stem: 10 friends form a large firm under the 1994 Act. Q: What type of organization?
mcqData.explanation:
Any organization formed under the 1994 Company Act is a Joint Stock firm.

Correct Answer: Joint Stock

mcqData.text: Stem: Jalil, Khalil, and 3 friends form a legal entity. They now want to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial type?
mcqData.explanation:
Small group (5 people) + legal status = Private Ltd company.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To implement the new decision (go Public): (i. Inc. members, ii. Prospectus, iii. Commencement Cert)
mcqData.explanation:
Standard procedure for converting from Private to Public Ltd to allow public share sales.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Disadvantages of Joint Stock business: (i. Separation of ownership & mgmt, ii. Legal obligations, iii. Complexity in decision making)
mcqData.explanation:
All three are inherent challenges: legal formalities are high, and directors make decisions, not the owners directly.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: For which type of share is the price NOT paid in cash?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are free shares issued to existing shareholders from accumulated profits.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Stem: 'Nosha Fashion' is formed under the 1994 Act. Members' liability is limited by shares. Mr. Robi bought shares that allow him to be a director. Q: What type of firm?
mcqData.explanation:
Only Public Ltd. companies can be implied when the prompt mentions a broader scope of share purchase for directorship.

Correct Answer: Public Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What type of share did Mr. Robi buy?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Ordinary shares give the right to vote and the eligibility to become a director.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: (Duplicate) For which share is no payment required?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are distributed free of cost.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Jamal and friend formed a firm under the 1994 Act with max 50 members. Now they want to change structure to raise capital. Q: Initial firm type?
mcqData.explanation:
A company under the 1994 Act with a limit of 50 members is a Private Limited Company.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Steps to implement the change (Private to Public): (i. Inc. members, ii. Inc. directors, iii. Get commencement letter)
mcqData.explanation:
All three are legal requirements for converting to and operating as a Public Ltd. company.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Who elects directors in a newly formed company?
mcqData.explanation:
In the first instance (before the first AGM), directors are usually appointed by the promoters.

Correct Answer: Promoters

mcqData.text: How many clauses are in the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) There are 6 mandatory clauses in the MoA.

Correct Answer: 6

mcqData.text: For an investor prioritizing only dividend income, which share is best?
mcqData.explanation:
Preference shares offer a fixed dividend rate and priority in payment over others.

Correct Answer: Preference

mcqData.text: Distributing shares instead of cash dividends is called?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) This is the definition of a Bonus Share.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Changing which clause of the MoA requires government/court permission?
mcqData.explanation:
While address changes are easier, changing the Name or Object requires stricter legal approval.

Correct Answer: Name

mcqData.text: Within how many days must minimum capital be collected after issuing the prospectus?
mcqData.explanation:
Law requires that the minimum subscription must be raised within 120 days.

Correct Answer: 120 days

mcqData.text: Stem: R company bought 60% shares of A company. Q: What type is R company?
mcqData.explanation:
The company that owns the majority stake (>50%) is the Holding company.

Correct Answer: Holding

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Why did R company make this decision? (i. Expansion, ii. Friendship, iii. Profit growth)
mcqData.explanation:
Holding/Subsidiary relationships are for financial and strategic expansion, not personal friendship.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: What is the year of the prevailing Company Act in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The Companies Act of 1994 is the current legal framework.

Correct Answer: 1994

mcqData.text: Advantages of a company: (i. Limited liability, ii. Efficient management, iii. Autocratic leadership)
mcqData.explanation:
Autocratic leadership is a disadvantage; companies favor democratic or professional management.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: Friends registered a firm with independent status. Now they are inviting the public to buy shares. Q: Initial firm type?
mcqData.explanation:
An independent legal entity that transitions to "inviting the public" was initially a Private Ltd.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What added benefit does the new Public structure offer? (i. Free share transfer, ii. No legal limits, iii. Public trust)
mcqData.explanation:
Public companies face more legal limits, not fewer; however, shares become easily transferable.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Contents of the Memorandum (MoA) include: (i. Rules/Articles, ii. Object, iii. Name)
mcqData.explanation:
"Rules/Policy" (সংঘবিধি) belong to the Articles of Association, not the MoA.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: How many types of limited liability companies are there?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited by shares and Limited by guarantee.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Which type of shareholder is the actual owner?
mcqData.explanation:
General (Ordinary) shareholders possess voting rights and bear the ultimate risk.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. X plays a lead role in forming a company. His shares will cover preliminary expenses. Q: What type of shares?
mcqData.explanation:
Promoters (প্রবর্তক) receive these shares for their work and to cover initial costs.

Correct Answer: Promoter's

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Characteristics of the business? (i. Artificial entity, ii. Law-created, iii. Unlimited liability)
mcqData.explanation:
Joint stock companies have limited liability, not unlimited.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: 'New Fashion' is registered but cannot start work without a key document. Q: Which one?
mcqData.explanation:
Public companies must obtain the Certificate of Commencement to start operations.

Correct Answer: Commencement Letter

mcqData.text: Minimum directors in a Private Ltd. Co.?
mcqData.explanation:
Legal minimum is 2 for Private and 3 for Public Ltd companies.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Which is the 4th clause of the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
Sequence: Name, Office, Object, Liability, Capital, Consent.

Correct Answer: Liability

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Sunny and 10 friends initiate 'Food Processing Private Ltd.' Q: Sunny's primary identity?
mcqData.explanation:
Those who take the initiative to form a company are called Promoters (প্রবর্তক).

Correct Answer: Promoter

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Sunny's duties to start the firm? (i. Registration, ii. Min. Capital, iii. Financial planning)
mcqData.explanation:
Promoters handle legal registration, capital planning, and financial blueprints.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Which is the "Constitution" of a company?
mcqData.explanation:
The Memorandum (স্মারকলিপি) is the primary governing document.

Correct Answer: Memorandum

mcqData.text: Within how many days must minimum capital be raised?
mcqData.explanation:
Correction Note: Law typically states 120 days from prospectus; 180 days is often cited in older texts or specific bank contexts.

Correct Answer: 180

mcqData.text: Minimum promoters needed for a Public Ltd. Co. at the initiative stage?
mcqData.explanation:
The minimum number of members (7) must act as promoters to start a Public Co.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: Which business has an artificial legal personality?
mcqData.explanation:
The suffix "Ltd." indicates a company incorporated by law with artificial personality.

Correct Answer: S.M. Ltd.

mcqData.text: Which is the 1st clause of the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
The Name Clause (নাম ধারা) is always the first clause.

Correct Answer: Name

mcqData.text: Companies formed by a Presidential Ordinance are?
mcqData.explanation:
Statutory (সংবিধিবদ্ধ) companies are created by special legislative acts or ordinances.

Correct Answer: Statutory

mcqData.text: Where CANNOT a Private Ltd. Co. raise funds from?
mcqData.explanation:
Private Ltd. companies are legally barred from selling shares to the general public/stock market.

Correct Answer: Stock Market

mcqData.text: For which share is the price NOT paid in cash?
mcqData.explanation:
Bonus shares are free of cost, issued from existing profits.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Which shares give priority to existing shareholders during distribution?
mcqData.explanation:
Right Shares (অধিকারযোগ্য শেয়ার) are offered first to existing owners.

Correct Answer: Right

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Jamal buys 5,000 shares. He receives a fixed 10% profit annually. Q: What type of share?
mcqData.explanation:
A fixed percentage of profit (dividend) is a feature of Preference shares.

Correct Answer: Preference

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Minimum directors for 'Bonolata Co.' (Public Co)?
mcqData.explanation:
Public Limited companies must have at least 3 directors.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Possesses an independent legal entity despite not being a person: (i. Mainamati Ent., ii. Upolota Ltd., iii. Gomoti Pvt. Ltd.)
mcqData.explanation:
Only companies with "Ltd." or "Pvt. Ltd." are incorporated entities with artificial personality.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: Which Company Act is currently prevalent in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) The Companies Act of 1994 is the governing law.

Correct Answer: 1994

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a clause of the Memorandum?
mcqData.explanation:
Management rules belong to the Articles of Association, not the Memorandum (MoA).

Correct Answer: Management

mcqData.text: Minimum number of directors in a Public Ltd. Co.?
mcqData.explanation:
Legal minimum is 3 for Public and 2 for Private companies.

Correct Answer: 3

mcqData.text: Stem: X Ltd. earned big profits but needs new investment. Shareholders want dividends. Directors find loans risky. Q: Best way to raise capital?
mcqData.explanation:
By issuing Bonus Shares, the company keeps the cash for investment while shareholders get "dividends" in the form of shares.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) To set up a garment factory with its own cloth?
mcqData.explanation:
If the original Object Clause in the Memorandum (MoA) doesn't mention garments, it must be amended.

Correct Answer: MoA Amend

mcqData.text: Formed by a Presidential Ordinance?
mcqData.explanation:
Statutory (সংবিধিবদ্ধ) companies are created by special legislative acts or ordinances.

Correct Answer: Statutory

mcqData.text: Where are the rules for using the company's official seal recorded?
mcqData.explanation:
Internal procedural rules like seal usage are in the Articles of Association (পরিমেল নিয়মাবলি).

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: Which debenture is sold for the "medium-term"?
mcqData.explanation:
Redeemable (পরিশোধযোগ্য) debentures are usually issued for a specific medium-term period.

Correct Answer: Redeemable

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Alam wants to build a firm that controls five other firms. Q: What is this called?
mcqData.explanation:
A company that controls others (usually through >50% shares) is a Holding Company.

Correct Answer: Holding

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Why would the public buy shares in Mr. Alam's firm? (i. Perpetual existence, ii. Limited liability, iii. Transferability)
mcqData.explanation:
These are the three main "Golden Characteristics" of a Public Limited Company.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Selling shares is better than selling debentures because: (i. No repayment till winding up, ii. No interest if no profit)
mcqData.explanation:
Unlike loans, share capital isn't a debt and doesn't require fixed interest payments.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which account is needed to invest in company shares?
mcqData.explanation:
A Beneficiary Owner (B.O.) account is mandatory for trading shares in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: B.O.

mcqData.text: Dividend paid as shares instead of cash?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) This is the definition of a Bonus Share.

Correct Answer: Bonus

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Kabir bought 1000 shares of Lanka Ltd. at the Dhaka Stock Exchange. Q: What type of company?
mcqData.explanation:
Only Public Ltd. companies are listed and traded on Stock Exchanges (DSE/CSE).

Correct Answer: Public Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) If Lanka Ltd. goes bankrupt, what is Mr. Kabir's liability?
mcqData.explanation:
Liability is limited to the face value of shares (1000 shares $\times$ 100 TK = 1,00,000). Market price is irrelevant here.

Correct Answer: Up to 1,00,000

mcqData.text: Which business can issue shares in the market?
mcqData.explanation:
Only Public Ltd. companies have the legal right to invite the general public to buy shares.

Correct Answer: Public Ltd.

mcqData.text: Which document concerns "Internal Management"?
mcqData.explanation:
Articles of Association/সংঘবিধি governs internal rules.

Correct Answer: Articles (সংঘবিধি)

mcqData.text: What does "Limited" (Ltd) in a company name mean?
mcqData.explanation:
It signifies that shareholders are only liable for the amount unpaid on their shares.

Correct Answer: Limited Liability

mcqData.text: Which is a disadvantage of a Joint Stock Company?
mcqData.explanation:
The formation process involves many legal stages and documentation.

Correct Answer: Complex Formation

mcqData.text: Stem: Roni, Joni, Moni & 3 friends form a legal entity with 10cr capital. Later, they decide to sell shares to the public. Q: Initial firm type?
mcqData.explanation:
A small group (6 people) with a legal entity status is initially a Private Ltd company.

Correct Answer: Private Ltd.

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Steps to implement the new decision (going public): (i. MoA amendment, ii. Increase members, iii. Get commencement letter)
mcqData.explanation:
Converting to a Public Ltd. requires increasing members (min 7), MoA updates, and the legal right to start business.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Max members in a Private Limited company?
mcqData.explanation:
By law, a Private Limited company cannot have more than 50 members.

Correct Answer: 50

mcqData.text: Which type of shareholder is the actual owner?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) General/Ordinary shareholders are the true owners with voting rights.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: Hasin Ltd has 5 directors. Jahara Ltd gains power to appoint 3 of them. Q: What type of company is Hasin Ltd?
mcqData.explanation:
Since Jahara Ltd controls the board, Hasin Ltd is the Subsidiary (অধীনস্থ) company.

Correct Answer: Subsidiary

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) What must Mr. Isham do to be appointed as a director?
mcqData.explanation:
To be a director, one must own a minimum amount of shares known as Qualification Shares (যোগ্যতাসূচক শেয়ার).

Correct Answer: Buy Qual. Shares

mcqData.text: Which is a disadvantage of Joint Stock business?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) The legal process of formation is very long and complex.

Correct Answer: Complex formation

mcqData.text: Which Company Act is currently used in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) The Companies Act of 1994 is the primary law.

Correct Answer: 1994
